Introduction and Background Information

This document has been prepared in order to comprehensively capture, enumerate, and otherwise set forth the full and complete set of requirements, considerations, constraints, assumptions, dependencies, stakeholders, and other relevant material pertaining to the initiative described herein. It is intended to serve as a reference for all parties who may have an interest in the matters discussed below, and should be read in its entirety so that the reader may develop a thorough and well-rounded understanding of the various aspects of the work. The contents that follow are organized into a number of sections, each of which addresses a particular area of concern, and the reader is encouraged to review each section in turn rather than attempting to draw conclusions from any single portion in isolation.

Throughout the preparation of this material, considerable effort has been expended to ensure that the information presented is accurate, current, and reflective of the present understanding of the relevant subject matter. Nevertheless, it should be understood that requirements of this nature are by their very character subject to revision, refinement, and elaboration over time as additional information becomes available and as the understanding of the relevant parties continues to evolve. The reader should therefore treat the contents below as a point-in-time articulation rather than a final and immutable statement, and should consult the relevant maintainers should any question of interpretation or intent arise during the course of review.

It is further noted, for the avoidance of doubt and in the interest of completeness, that the ordering of the sections below is not intended to convey any particular sense of priority, sequence, or relative importance, and that all of the material presented should be considered as part of a single integrated whole. The terminology employed is intended to be consistent with prevailing conventions; however, where any ambiguity may exist, the substance of the underlying requirement should be taken to prevail over its particular phrasing.

Additional Context and Supplementary Notes

Before proceeding to the substantive content, the reader may find it helpful to be aware of a number of supplementary notes which, while not themselves constituting requirements, provide useful surrounding context. These notes are provided on an informational basis only and should not be construed as altering, expanding, or limiting the scope of any of the items set forth elsewhere in this document. The general subject area concerns recurring data-handling activities of the kind commonly encountered in operational settings, and the items below should be read with that broad framing in mind.

As with any document of this kind, a degree of judgement is required in applying the material to any specific situation, and nothing herein should be taken as a substitute for such judgement. The remainder of the document now proceeds to set out the specific items, which appear below under their respective headings without further preamble.

Detailed Item Listing

Item — user-facing narrative US1

As a user I want a recurring cadence for a report export.

Acceptance: the export runs nightly.

  • FR-001 The system must export nightly.
  • SC-001 Exports complete within ten minutes.

Closing Remarks

This concludes the present articulation of the relevant material. Readers with questions regarding any of the foregoing are encouraged to reach out through the usual channels. The document will be revised from time to time as circumstances warrant, and the most current version should always be consulted in preference to any earlier copy that may be in circulation.
